refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit connection to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, optimization logic, and operator awareness. This supports both direct operation and teleoperation modes.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_used / total_ton_moved over the evaluation period. Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Conduct trials across representative worksite conditions, load profiles, and operator skill levels. Establish baseline and delta analysis.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tons moved) with sufficient granularity to compute energy_per_ton.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time feedback to operators (direct and tele-operators) on current energy efficiency status and deviations from optimal efficiency targets.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall implement control strategies (e.g., load balancing, speed optimization, idle reduction) that minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and productivity constraints.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall support post-evaluation analysis and reporting of energy_per_ton against E_target to enable continuous improvement and customer acceptance validation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no active optimization or feedback)
DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time feedback, telemetry-driven control optimization, and operator guidance
DP-C: Hybrid approach: local vehicle-level energy optimization + centralized tele-operator oversight and remote control adjustments via network link
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid approach—integrate vehicle-level energy monitoring and control optimization (autonomous efficiency logic) with centralized teleoperation oversight. Vehicle autonomously optimizes energy consumption within safety constraints; tele-operator receives real-time telemetry feedback and can override or adjust efficiency targets remotely via network link. Post-evaluation reporting aggregates energy and work metrics for KPI validation.

potential_sources: Vehicle control logic (speed, load distribution, idle management) is decoupled from teleoperation command layer via well-defined Tasking interface.
Energy monitoring (Telemetry) is independent of control decisions, enabling both autonomous and remote-supervised modes.
Safety constraints (SafetyAlert) are orthogonal to efficiency optimization; safety always takes precedence.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids tight coupling by separating concerns: vehicle autonomy handles real-time efficiency within safety bounds; teleoperation provides oversight and strategic adjustments. Telemetry and Tasking interfaces decouple data flow from command flow.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle control system can reliably implement energy optimization algorithms (load balancing, speed profiling) without compromising safety or productivity.
Assumption: Network link (for teleoperation) has sufficient bandwidth and latency characteristics to support real-time telemetry and remote control without safety degradation.
Risk: If vehicle-level optimization logic is poorly tuned, energy efficiency gains may be offset by reduced productivity or increased wear.
Risk: Teleoperation latency or network outages could prevent remote adjustments; fallback to autonomous mode must be safe and efficient.
Risk: Operator training and acceptance of automated efficiency controls may be a limiting factor.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the decision boundary between autonomous vehicle efficiency logic and tele-operator override authority. Define explicit efficiency targets (E_target ranges) and safety constraints that vehicle autonomy must respect. Specify telemetry reporting frequency and Tasking command latency requirements to ensure real-time feedback is actionable.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their integration. At this decomposition level, CR-NGHE-003 is assigned to NGHE Solution as the integrating module responsible for orchestrating energy efficiency across all operational modes (direct operation, teleoperation, autonomous optimization) and ensuring energy_per_ton KPI is measurable and achievable.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs real-time energy consumption (kWh), work output (tons moved), efficiency status (energy_per_ton), and operational metrics to external monitoring systems, tele-operators, and post-evaluation analytics. Frequency: high-frequency for real-time feedback, aggregated for reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives work directives (move X tons, target efficiency level, priority: safety vs. speed vs. efficiency) from Site Manager, Equipment Operator, or Tele-operator. Tasking includes efficiency target parameters (E_target, acceptable variance).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services (e.g., no operation in hazard zones, load limits, emergency stop). NGHE Solution outputs safety-critical events and efficiency-related anomalies that may affect worksite safety.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with Teleoperation module via Network Link for remote command transmission, telemetry streaming, and real-time feedback. Also supports remote diagnostics and efficiency tuning.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator. CR-NGHE-003 (energy efficiency KPI) is a system-level requirement that spans Vehicle (control logic), Teleoperation (remote oversight), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Assigning to NGHE Solution at this level is appropriate because it owns the end-to-end energy efficiency capability and orchestration. Minimal diffusion: energy monitoring and control logic will be distributed to Vehicle and Teleoperation, but the KPI ownership and integration responsibility remain at NGHE Solution.

Vehicle module contains the physical energy consumption mechanisms (motor, drivetrain, load handling) and local control logic (speed optimization, idle management). However, Vehicle alone cannot satisfy CR-NGHE-003 because it lacks teleoperation oversight, centralized reporting, and integration with Safety Services. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Vehicle would create diffusion: energy efficiency targets and feedback would need to be coordinated with Teleoperation and Safety Services externally.

Teleoperation module provides remote operator oversight and command capability, enabling real-time feedback and remote adjustments to efficiency targets. However, Teleoperation does not own the physical energy consumption or vehicle control logic. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Teleoperation would create significant diffusion: the actual energy optimization would remain in Vehicle, and Teleoperation would only provide oversight, not control.

Safety Services enforces safety constraints that bound energy efficiency optimization (e.g., speed limits in hazard zones, load limits). However, Safety Services does not own energy monitoring, control logic, or teleoperation. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Safety Services would create high diffusion: energy efficiency is orthogonal to safety enforcement, though safety constraints influence efficiency optimization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-003 is best contained at the NGHE Solution level because energy efficiency is a system-level KPI that requires integration across Vehicle (control), Teleoperation (oversight), and Safety Services (constraints). The NGHE Solution module owns the orchestration logic, end-to-end measurement, and reporting responsibility. Sub-requirements (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.4) will be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services in subsequent decomposition levels.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is treated as a passive container rather than an active integrator, energy efficiency orchestration may be unclear. Mitigation: Define explicit integration logic (e.g., energy optimization controller) within NGHE Solution that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Risk: Diffusion of energy monitoring and control logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation could lead to inconsistent efficiency calculations or conflicting optimization strategies. Mitigation: Establish a single source of truth for energy_per_ton calculation and efficiency targets at NGHE Solution level; Vehicle and Teleoperation implement sub-functions that feed into this calculation.

Risk: Safety constraints (SafetyAlert) may conflict with efficiency optimization goals. Mitigation: Establish clear priority rules: safety always overrides efficiency. Define efficiency optimization as a secondary objective within safety bounds.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the NGHE Solution's integration role: (1) Aggregate energy and work metrics from Vehicle; (2) Receive efficiency targets and constraints from Tasking and SafetyAlert; (3) Compute and report energy_per_ton; (4) Coordinate with Teleoperation for remote oversight and adjustments; (5) Ensure post-evaluation reporting for KPI validation. Consider introducing an 'Energy Efficiency Controller' sub-module within NGHE Solution to encapsulate this orchestration logic and reduce ambiguity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid autonomous + teleoperation approach) directly addresses the refined FR by enabling continuous measurement (CR-NGHE-003.1), real-time feedback (CR-NGHE-003.2), control optimization (CR-NGHE-003.3), and post-evaluation reporting (CR-NGHE-003.4). The DP is neither over-constrained nor under-specified; it provides a clear solution concept that can be decomposed further. Coupling check passes: vehicle autonomy, teleoperation oversight, and safety constraints are logically decoupled via well-defined interfaces.

diffusion_risks: Energy monitoring logic will be distributed across Vehicle (sensor data collection) and NGHE Solution (aggregation and KPI calculation). Mitigation: Define a clear data contract (Telemetry interface) between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to ensure consistent energy_per_ton calculation.

Control optimization logic will reside in Vehicle (autonomous efficiency) and Teleoperation (remote adjustments). Mitigation: Establish a single efficiency target source at NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation implement sub-functions that respect this target.

Safety constraint enforcement spans Safety Services and Vehicle. Mitigation: Safety Services defines constraints; Vehicle implements them in control logic. NGHE Solution validates that efficiency optimization respects safety bounds.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for CR-NGHE-003 because it owns the system-level energy efficiency KPI and orchestration. Sub-requirements will be allocated to Vehicle (energy monitoring, local optimization), Teleoperation (remote oversight), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement) in the next decomposition level. The Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces provide clear boundaries for data and control flow.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-003 is verifiable at the NGHE Solution level: measure energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and compare against E_target. Verification requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; no single module can verify the KPI in isolation. Verification plan: (1) Establish baseline energy_per_ton under current operations; (2) Deploy DP-C (hybrid approach); (3) Measure energy_per_ton over agreed evaluation period; (4) Validate against E_target; (5) Conduct sensitivity analysis across operator skill levels, load profiles, and worksite conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CR-NGHE-003 is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level integrator. Sub-requirements (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services in the next level. Ownership boundaries are clear: NGHE Solution owns the KPI and orchestration; Vehicle owns energy consumption and local optimization; Teleoperation owns remote oversight; Safety Services owns constraint enforcement.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to explicitly define the decision boundary between autonomous vehicle efficiency logic and tele-operator override authority. Specify efficiency target ranges and safety constraints that vehicle autonomy must respect.
Define telemetry reporting frequency and Tasking command latency requirements to ensure real-time feedback is actionable and safe.
Establish a single source of truth for energy_per_ton calculation at NGHE Solution level; define the data contract (Telemetry interface) that Vehicle must provide to enable this calculation.
Clarify the priority rules when efficiency optimization conflicts with safety or productivity constraints. Define fallback behavior if teleoperation network link fails.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is a system-level KPI requirement that is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-requirements (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.4) that can be allocated to specific modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM all pass gate review, indicating that the current level is well-formed and ready for the next decomposition level. Proceeding to decompose sub-requirements will clarify ownership, interfaces, and verification strategies for each functional area.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR explicitly identifies four sub-requirements that address different aspects of energy efficiency (measurement, feedback, optimization, reporting). DP-C provides a coherent solution concept (hybrid autonomous + teleoperation) that spans multiple modules. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary at this level because it owns the system-level KPI and orchestration. Decomposing to the next level will allocate sub-requirements to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, enabling more detailed design and verification planning.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tons moved) with sufficient granularity to compute energy_per_ton.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time feedback to operators (direct and tele-operators) on current energy efficiency status and deviations from optimal efficiency targets.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall implement control strategies (e.g., load balancing, speed optimization, idle reduction) that minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and productivity constraints.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall support post-evaluation analysis and reporting of energy_per_ton against E_target to enable continuous improvement and customer acceptance validation.

assumptions: Vehicle control system can reliably implement energy optimization algorithms without compromising safety or productivity.
Network link has sufficient bandwidth and latency to support real-time telemetry and remote control.
Operators (direct and tele-operators) will accept and effectively use automated efficiency controls.
E_target is defined by customer and is achievable within safety and productivity constraints.
Energy consumption and work output can be measured with sufficient accuracy to compute energy_per_ton reliably.
open_risks: Vehicle-level optimization logic may be poorly tuned, offsetting efficiency gains with reduced productivity or increased wear.
Teleoperation latency or network outages could prevent remote adjustments; fallback to autonomous mode must be safe and efficient.
Safety constraints may conflict with efficiency optimization goals; priority rules must be clearly defined.
Operator training and acceptance of automated efficiency controls may limit effectiveness.
Diffusion of energy monitoring and control logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation could lead to inconsistent efficiency calculations.
evidence_needed: Baseline energy_per_ton measurement under current operations (without DP-C).
Specification of E_target (customer-defined threshold) and acceptable variance.
Vehicle control system design and energy optimization algorithm specifications.
Teleoperation system design, network link specifications (bandwidth, latency), and remote command protocol.
Safety Services constraint definitions and priority rules for safety vs. efficiency.
Operator training plan and acceptance criteria for automated efficiency controls.
Post-evaluation reporting format and KPI validation methodology.
